author: Selfish
text:
Tag delivered_in with delivered_repo provenance

A closed task's delivered_in sha is resolvable only against whichever client repo produced it; on a shared hub (bl-ffb4) or a sibling client that pointer is opaque. Pair every sha write with a delivered_repo string — the local clone's origin URL (else basename, else path) — so a reader knows where to fetch from.

Wired in both delivery paths: review_safety::commit_squash_and_flip (local-squash) and delivery::populate_on_close (deferred, including --delivered). Reused task.repo's existing identity shape by lifting repo_identity/git_origin_url/identity_from out of commands/basic.rs into crate::repo_url; both write sites and the bl create site now share one helper.

Forward-compat: pre-bl-7523 tasks without the field load as null. The agreed reading is 'delivered against the locally-checked-out repo,' so single-repo deployments stay byte-identical and no retrofit runs. bl show surfaces a 'delivered repo:' line; --json carries delivered_repo on the task as a first-class field.
